description: Framework registry mapping OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025, v2.0) and MITRE ATLAS technique IDs to their canonical URLs and summaries. Each threat in data/security-threats.json references framework IDs from this registry via its 'frameworks' object. CWE links use a URL pattern against cwe.mitre.org. IMPORTANT: Every URL, title, and summary in this file was authored from the model's training-time knowledge and MUST be manually verified against the official sources listed under 'sources' before being treated as authoritative. Flip verificationRequired to false once each entry has been reviewed.

owasp-llm.name: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications
owasp-llm.verificationNote: Open the landing page and compare each LLMxx:2025 ID, title and slug. The per-risk URL pattern is https://genai.owasp.org/llmrisk/<slug>/ — confirm each slug exactly before trusting the link.

mitre-atlas.name: MITRE ATLAS (Adversarial Threat Landscape for Artificial-Intelligence Systems)
mitre-atlas.verificationNote: Technique URLs follow https://atlas.mitre.org/techniques/AML.TXXXX. Verify each ID resolves and the title still matches — ATLAS revises names and merges techniques periodically.

cwe.name: Common Weakness Enumeration (MITRE)
cwe.verificationNote: CWE IDs are stable; URL pattern has not changed in years. Safe to trust the pattern, but confirm that each CWE ID referenced in security-threats.json is still the canonical one for the weakness described.

LLM01:2025.title: Prompt Injection
LLM01:2025.summary: User or system inputs manipulate the LLM into executing unintended instructions, bypassing guardrails, or leaking data.

LLM02:2025.title: Sensitive Information Disclosure
LLM02:2025.summary: The model reveals confidential data via completions, context leakage, or indexed source material.

LLM03:2025.title: Supply Chain
LLM03:2025.summary: Compromised models, datasets, plugins, or extensions introduce vulnerabilities into the LLM stack.

LLM05:2025.title: Improper Output Handling
LLM05:2025.summary: Downstream systems trust LLM output without validation — leading to XSS, SSRF, code execution, or license contamination.

LLM06:2025.title: Excessive Agency
LLM06:2025.summary: The LLM is granted tools, permissions, or autonomy beyond what is strictly required — amplifying the impact of any other failure.

LLM07:2025.title: System Prompt Leakage
LLM07:2025.summary: The system prompt or embedded context leaks to untrusted parties, exposing secrets, policies, or prior conversation data.

AML.T0010.title: ML Supply Chain Compromise
AML.T0010.summary: Adversaries tamper with ML models, datasets, or software dependencies along the supply chain to gain an initial foothold.

AML.T0050.title: Command and Scripting Interpreter
AML.T0050.summary: Adversaries abuse command and script interpreters — often via tool-using agents — to execute arbitrary code.

AML.T0051.title: LLM Prompt Injection
AML.T0051.summary: Adversarial instructions injected into prompts, documents, issues, or tool outputs alter LLM behavior.

AML.T0053.title: LLM Plugin Compromise
AML.T0053.summary: Adversaries leverage untrusted or compromised plugins/tools/extensions invoked by the LLM to exfiltrate data or escalate privilege.

AML.T0054.title: LLM Jailbreak
AML.T0054.summary: Adversaries bypass alignment or policy controls to elicit unsafe or prohibited model behaviors.

AML.T0057.title: LLM Data Leakage
AML.T0057.summary: Sensitive information from training, context, or tool responses leaks to unauthorized parties via the LLM.

cwePattern.note: Strip the 'CWE-' prefix when substituting {id}. Example: CWE-200 → https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/200.html
